title: Staff Engineer @ Meta by Age 25 | Evan King
guest_role: Ex-Meta Staff Engineer (IC6 by age 25), later startup founder
key_takeaways: Evan went from Junior (IC3) to Staff (IC6) at Meta in three years, reaching Staff by age 25.
Both Evan and host Ryan identify as 'code machines' - high-output engineers rather than natural prodigies; Evan started programming late in high school.
His manager handed him borderline engineering-management responsibilities to scale a growing team, and soft skills from founding Cornell's Hacking Club proved instrumental.
Impact trumps technical complexity: his Staff promotion came from a simple project with extraordinary results, not technical sophistication.
He was promoted in his first half by pursuing projects outside his core scope, showing agency in seeking opportunities.
His Senior-to-Staff case came from leading the creation of Meta's real-time integrity team after the Christchurch incident, combining visibility and impact.
He argues startups promote faster technical growth than big tech: big tech offers depth, startups provide breadth.
An after-hours crypto side project gained 10k+ users and eventually led him to leave big tech for entrepreneurship.
He regrets over-prioritizing work early on at the cost of friendships and self-reflection, and taking promotion shortcuts that sacrificed deep technical understanding he later needed.

notable_quotes.speaker: Evan King
notable_quotes.quote: Startups promote faster technical growth than big tech does.
